zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret Harleston於大約1456年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可巴德維爾時，她的父親John Harleston27歲，母親Margaret Bardwell27歲。她於1477年與Thomas Darcy在英國素福爾可結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1489年9月18日在英國英格蘭艾可馬爾頓享年34歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可馬爾頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret Harleston于大约1456年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可巴德維爾时，她的父亲John Harleston27岁，母亲Margaret Bardwell27岁。她于1477年与Thomas Darcy在英国素福爾可结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1489年9月18日在英国英格兰艾可馬爾頓享年34岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可馬爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Harleston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (West Lothian) and English: patronymic, with intrusive -t-, from the personal name Charles (compare Charleson ). The various places called Charleston are all of recent origin, so they are unlikely to be the source of the surname.

variants.name: Charles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Antoine, Andre, Francois, Patrice, Germaine, Magalie, Philippe, Yves, Alain, Jacques, Cecile.

French, Welsh, English, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and African (mainly Tanzania): from the French form of the ancient Germanic personal name Karl ‘man, husband, freeman’, which was Latinized as Carolus. In France the personal name was popular from an early date, due to the fame of the Emperor Charlemagne (c. 742–814; Latin name Carolus Magnus, i.e. Charles the Great). The Old French form Charles was briefly introduced to England by the Normans, but was rare during the main period of surname formation. It was introduced more successfully to Scotland in the 16th century by the Stuarts, who had strong ties with France, and was brought by them to England in the 17th century. Its frequency as a Welsh surname is attributable to the late date of Welsh surname formation. Old English Ceorl ‘peasant’ is also found as a byname, but the resulting Middle English form, Charl, with a patronymic in -s, if it existed at all, would have been absorbed by the French form introduced by the Normans. English variants pronounced with initial k- for the most part reflect the cognate Old Norse personal name Karl, Karli. Compare Carl .

English: in some cases, possibly a habitational name from Charles (Devon), or a post-medieval variant of Cherrill, with excrescent -s, perhaps by false association with the personal name Charles. Cherrill is either a habitational name from Cherhill in Wilshire, or a status name from Middle English cherl, cheril, charl, chirl ‘churl, bonded tenant, serf; peasant’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Harleston was born about 1456, in Bardwell, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Harleston, was 27 and her mother, Margaret Bardwell, was 27. She married Thomas Darcy in 1477, in Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 18 September 1489, in Maldon, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 34, and was buried in Maldon, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret Harleston est née environ 1456, à Bardwell, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Harleston, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Margaret Bardwell, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Darcy en 1477, à Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 18 septembre 1489, à Maldon, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 34 ans, et a été enterrée à Maldon, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Margaret Harleston um 1456 in Bardwell, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Harleston 27 und ihre Mutter Margaret Bardwell 27. Sie hat 1477 in Suffolk, England Thomas Darcy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 18. September 1489 im Alter von 34 Jahren in Maldon, Essex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Maldon, Essex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Harleston è nata il circa 1456 nel Bardwell, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, John Harleston, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Margaret Bardwell, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Darcy nel 1477, nel Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 18 settembre 1489, nel Maldon, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 34 anni, e fu sepolta a Maldon, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Harlestonが約1456年にイギリスイギリススフォルクバードウエルで生まれた時，父John Harlestonは27歳，母Margaret Bardwellは27歳であった。1477年にイギリススフォルクでThomas Darcyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1489年9月18日にイギリスイギリスエセックスマルドンで34歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスマルドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Harleston(이)가 대략 1456년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 바드웰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Harleston(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Bardwell(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1477년 영국 수폴크에서 Thomas Darcy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1489년 9월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 맬던에서 34세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 맬던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Harleston nasceu aproximadamente 1456, em Bardwell, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Harleston, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Bardwell, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Darcy em 1477, em Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 18 de setembro de 1489, em Maldon, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 34 anos, e foi sepultada em Maldon, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret Harleston родилась около 1456 г. в местности бардвелл, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Harleston было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Bardwell, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Darcy в 1477 г. в местности суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 18 сентября 1489 г. в местности малдон, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 34 лет и была похоронена в местности малдон, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Margaret Harleston nació aproximadamente 1456, en Bardwell, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Harleston, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Margaret Bardwell, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Thomas Darcy en 1477, en Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 18 de septiembre de 1489, en Maldon, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 34 años, y fue sepultada en Maldon, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
